market_summary: Broad market is under moderate pressure on June 11, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq pulling back amid risk-off sentiment; AI and chip stocks are catching a bid mid-session after Oracle reported strong earnings and announced ~$70B in data center spending plans for next year, partially offsetting broader weakness.

MSFT.headline: MSFT is down ~17% YTD and testing the lower boundary of a four-month trading range, with Azure layoffs in China adding near-term headline risk.
MSFT.reasoning: Microsoft is underperforming the S&P 500 significantly in 2026 (down ~17% vs. index up ~7%), driven largely by sentiment shifts rather than fundamental deterioration — Q3 2026 revenue grew 18.3% YoY to $82.9B and last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 5.18%. Azure now spans 500+ data centers globally and the next earnings report is July 28, which is the real catalyst to watch. On a price-to-cash-from-operations basis, MSFT hasn't been this cheap since 2019, and 29 analysts carry a median 12-month price target of $550 — no action needed, but the YTD underperformance warrants monitoring.

AMZN.headline: Amazon secured a $17.5B delayed-draw term loan facility arranged by Citigroup to fund accelerating AI infrastructure spending.
AMZN.reasoning: The $17.5B loan facility is a clear signal of Amazon's aggressive commitment to AI and AWS infrastructure buildout — a long-term positive for the cloud and AI thesis. AWS Graviton5 is now generally available, strengthening AWS's competitive position. Last quarter's EPS of $2.78 beat estimates by 70%, and 62 analysts rate AMZN a Strong Buy with a $312 average price target; the ~25% pullback from May highs looks like noise for a long-term holder.

NVDA.headline: NVDA is catching a pre-market bounce driven by Oracle's $70B data center spending commitment, reinforcing robust GPU demand for AI infrastructure.
NVDA.reasoning: Oracle's ~$70B capex announcement is a direct demand signal for Nvidia's high-performance GPUs, and Nvidia's own FY2026 revenue surged 65% to $215.9B. The stock is down from its May all-time high of $236.54 but remains up ~42% over the past year, and analysts carry a $298 average 12-month price target implying ~50% upside. The OpenAI 10-gigawatt Ohio data center project — which may include Nvidia financial backing — is a further long-term tailwind; no action warranted.

GOOGL.headline: Google is in talks with Samsung to co-manufacture its next-generation custom chip, deepening its silicon independence strategy.
GOOGL.reasoning: Today's ~2.3% decline is the sharpest in the portfolio but appears driven by broad market risk-off rather than any fundamental development — GOOGL's Q1 FY26 posted 22% YoY revenue growth and 81% net income growth with strong Google Cloud momentum. The Samsung chip partnership, if confirmed, would meaningfully reduce reliance on third-party silicon and expand AI infrastructure capabilities. A $0.22 quarterly dividend payment is due June 15, and 56 analysts maintain a Strong Buy with a $432 average price target.

alerts.message: MSFT is down ~17% YTD, trading near 4-month range lows, and Azure China layoffs add headline noise. Monitor Q3 earnings on July 28 as the next key fundamental checkpoint; no action warranted absent a guidance cut.

long_term_view: All four holdings remain structurally intact as the dominant AI infrastructure and platform layer — surging enterprise AI capex (evidenced by Oracle's $70B commitment and Amazon's $17.5B loan facility) directly benefits NVDA, AMZN/AWS, and MSFT/Azure. Today's broad pullback is noise; the long-term AI buildout thesis is strengthening, not weakening.
